description_long: This sculpture depicts a male figure dressed in Roman-style armor, standing atop a square pedestal adorned with relief carvings. The figure is clad in a gold breastplate and a red tunic, with a red cape draped over his shoulder. He holds a tall staff or standard topped with a golden eagle in his left hand, while his right arm is extended outward, as if gesturing or addressing an audience. The pedestal features detailed bas-relief scenes, likely representing historical or mythological events. The overall appearance suggests a commemorative or heroic theme, reminiscent of classical Roman statuary.
description: A Roman-style armored male figure with a red cape stands on a decorated pedestal, holding a staff topped with a golden eagle and gesturing outward.
description_view_0: The figure is seen from the left side, with the extended right arm and the staff clearly visible, and the front relief of the pedestal partially in view.
description_view_1: The back of the figure is visible, showing the red cape and gold armor, with the rear relief of the pedestal in full view.
description_view_2: The right side of the figure is shown, highlighting the outstretched arm and the side reliefs of the pedestal.
description_view_3: The front of the figure faces forward, with the right arm extended and the staff upright, and the main relief on the pedestal clearly visible.
attribute_subject_matter: Roman soldier or leader in classical attire
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a white background.
